canto_title: The Third Circle: The Gluttons and the Prophecy of Florence

scenes.summary: Dante contemplates the grief caused by the events of the previous canto.

scenes.scene_name: The Environment of the Third Circle
scenes.summary: A description of the eternal rain, hail, and snow that characterizes this level of Hell.
The terrifying physical features of the three-mouthed demon are detailed.
The wretched souls attempt to shield themselves from the storm and the beast.
Virgil subdues the hungry Cerberus by throwing earth into his mouths.
A comparison is drawn between the demon's eating habits and a hungry dog.
One particular soul emerges from the mud upon seeing the travelers.
The rising soul asks Dante to recognize him.
Dante expresses his difficulty in recognizing the soul due to his current distress.
The soul reveals himself as Ciacco and explains his sin of gluttony.
Dante asks about the political future and current discord of his home city.
Ciacco predicts a period of violent civil war fueled by three specific vices.
Dante asks about the ultimate fate of famous Florentine statesmen.
Ciacco explains that these great men are located in the deeper, darker circles of Hell.
Ciacco sinks back down into the muddy earth.
Virgil warns of the coming Last Judgment and the resurrection of the flesh.
Dante asks whether future torments will increase or decrease after the final judgment.
Virgil explains that the intensity of pain is tied to the perfection of the soul.
The travelers continue their descent and encounter the gatekeeper Pluto.
